name: MITRE.LIN.T1040.RULE Network Sniffing
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/usr/sbin/tcpdump -k siem-network-sniffing
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/usr/sbin/tshark -k siem-network-sniffing
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/usr/sbin/rawshark -k siem-network-sniffing
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/usr/sbin/wireshark -k siem-network-sniffing

Set correct path for your linux distro (check 'whereis' command)

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-network-sniffing
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1040.RULE Network Sniffing on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-network-sniffing
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list:
Event description: Network sniffing refers to using the network interface on a system to monitor or capture information sent over a wired or wireless connection. An adversary may place a network interface into promiscuous mode to passively access data in transit over the network, or use span ports to capture a larger amount of data.

See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1040
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
